collectiondata.overview:
Best Tour Packages for Kashmir from India – Experience Heaven on Earth


If you're searching for the best tour packages for Kashmir from India, you’ve arrived at the perfect guide. Kashmir, often referred to as "Heaven on Earth", offers a stunning blend of snow-capped mountains, serene lakes, and timeless charm. Whether you're planning a honeymoon, family trip, or adventure tour, our Kashmir Tour Packages from cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Pune promise a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

Why Visit Kashmir?


Snow-Capped Mountains and Scenic Valleys

The majestic Himalayas cradle Kashmir’s valleys in beauty. From the snowy slopes of Gulmarg to the tranquil backwaters of Dal Lake, nature showcases its grandeur in every season. Visit during April to June for blooming tulips and lush meadows; visit in December to February for snow-covered landscapes and winter sports.


Rich Culture, Handicrafts, and Cuisine

Top Kashmir Tour Packages from India
​Explore authentic Kashmiri culture through vibrant local bazaars, saffron fields, and traditional Wazwan feasts. Pashmina shawls, handwoven carpets, copperware, and dry fruits make for excellent souvenirs. Popular markets like Lal Chowk and Polo View Market in Srinagar offer unique shopping experiences.
​
Ideal Destination for Honeymoon, Family, and Adventure Tours
Our Kashmir family tour packages, affordable Kashmir tour packages for couples, and adventure-based itineraries ensure every traveler finds their dream holiday. From peaceful houseboats to adrenaline-filled ski trips, there’s something for all.


Top Kashmir Tour Packages from India


4 Days Kashmir Tour Package from India – Perfect Short Getaway

This short itinerary includes Srinagar’s iconic Dal Lake, Mughal Gardens, Shankaracharya Temple, and a half-day excursion to Gulmarg. It’s perfect for a quick trip from cities like Kolkata or Pune.


6 Days Kashmir Family Tour Package – Explore Srinagar to Pahalgam
Tailored for families, this tour covers Srinagar, Gulmarg, Pahalgam, and optional add-ons like Betaab Valley and Aru Valley. This package is popular among travelers from Mumbai and Delhi.


Romantic Kashmir Honeymoon Packages from India
Designed for couples, these budget-friendly Kashmir honeymoon packages include private houseboat stays, candlelight dinners, shikara rides, and romantic strolls through flower gardens. Custom add-ons like floral bedroom decor and snow activities are available.


Budget and Luxury Kashmir Tour Packages for Indian Travelers
Choose from basic budget itineraries to luxury all-inclusive experiences. Kashmir tour packages with price options starting from ₹11,999 up to ₹45,000+ depending on the number of nights, season, and accommodation type.

Key Destinations Covered in Kashmir Tour Packages

Srinagar – Houseboats, Dal Lake & Mughal Gardens
Float on the Dal Lake in a traditional wooden houseboat, enjoy a shikara ride through lotus fields, and visit Mughal Gardens like Shalimar Bagh and Nishat Bagh. Don’t miss sites like Hazratbal Shrine and Pari Mahal.

Gulmarg – Snow Adventures & Cable Car Rides
A paradise for snow lovers, Gulmarg offers skiing, snowboarding, and Asia’s highest cable car ride—the Gulmarg Gondola. Best visited in winter (December to February).

Sonmarg – The Gateway to Ladakh
Surrounded by glaciers and green valleys, Sonmarg is perfect for trekking and a key base point for journeys toward Leh. Access may be limited in heavy winter months.

Inclusions in the Best Kashmir Tour Packages from India

Comfortable Accommodation & Meals
Tour packages include stays in hotels or houseboats with daily breakfast and dinner. Options range from 3-star to luxury boutique stays, with the flexibility to customize.

Local Transfers, Sightseeing, and Houseboat Stays
Packages include airport pickup/drop, private cabs or shared transfers, houseboat stays in Srinagar, and all major sightseeing with optional local guides.

Optional Add-ons: Skiing, Trekking, and Shikara Rides

Personalize your experience with thrilling add-ons like:

Gondola rides in Gulmarg
Rafting in Pahalgam
Shikara rides on Dal Lake
Snow treks and sledding in winter

Best Time to Visit Kashmir from India

Summer for Blooming Gardens and Mild Weather
April to June is the best time to enjoy the Tulip Festival, green meadows, and pleasant weather. Ideal for sightseeing and family trips.

Winter for Snow Activities and Scenic Landscapes
December to February is perfect for skiing, snowboarding, and cozy winter getaways. Great for honeymooners and adventure seekers.

Travel Tips for Each Season

Summer: Carry sunscreen, sunglasses, and light woolens
Winter: Layer up with thermals, gloves, snow boots, and jackets
Monsoon: Travel with raincoats and non-slip shoes; enjoy discounted prices

Booking 45–60 days in advance is recommended for peak seasons to secure good deals.

Frequently Asked Questions About Kashmir Tour Packages from India

Is Kashmir safe for tourists from India?
Yes, tourist areas like Srinagar, Gulmarg, and Pahalgam are safe and regularly visited. Always check official travel advisories before planning your trip.

How much does a typical Kashmir tour package cost?
Kashmir tour packages with price range from ₹11,999 (budget, 3N/4D without flights) to ₹45,000+ for deluxe stays and longer itineraries. Prices vary by season and inclusions.

Can I customize my Kashmir package based on my preferences?
Yes, all packages are customizable. You can add or remove destinations, choose hotel categories, add activities, or adjust for budget or travel style.

Are there affordable Kashmir tour packages for couples?
Yes, affordable Kashmir tour packages for couples include private stays, romantic sightseeing, and experiences like candlelight dinners and shikara rides—all within a reasonable budget.

Do you offer budget-friendly Kashmir honeymoon packages?
Yes. Our budget-friendly Kashmir honeymoon packages include comfortable hotels, honeymoon decor, houseboat stays, and couple-friendly sightseeing, starting at ₹17,999.

What about affordable Kashmir adventure tour packages?
Adventure seekers can opt for affordable Kashmir adventure tour packages with snow sports in Gulmarg, rafting in Pahalgam, and glacier treks in Sonmarg, all guided and budget-managed.

How to Book the Best Tour Package for Kashmir from India

Trusted Travel Agencies and Online Portals
Book only through verified travel agents or online platforms affiliated with the Ministry of Tourism. Look for customer reviews and transparency in inclusions.

Things to Check Before Finalizing Your Package

1. What’s included: meals, sightseeing, transport
2. Type of room: standard, deluxe, or houseboat
3. Cancellation policy and refund terms
4. Ratings and reviews of travel partners

Travel Documents and Packing Essentials

1. Valid photo ID (Aadhar, PAN, or Passport)
​2. Sufficient cash or UPI backup (rural ATMs may be limited)
3. Power bank, cold weather gear, first-aid kit
4. Foreign nationals must carry passports and may need extra registration

packages.overview:
Golden Temple – Witness the shimmering beauty of this sacred site, where spirituality and serenity merge in divine harmony.
Jallianwala Bagh – Step into history at this poignant memorial, echoing tales of courage and sacrifice.
Wagah Border Ceremony – Feel the patriotic fervor as India and Pakistan’s guards perform the electrifying Beating Retreat ceremony.
Vaishno Devi Trek – Embark on a soul-stirring pilgrimage through breathtaking mountain trails to seek blessings at the revered shrine.
Betaab Valley – Stroll through lush meadows surrounded by snow-capped peaks, a scene straight out of a fairytale.
Dal Lake Shikara Ride – Drift across glistening waters on a traditional wooden boat, absorbing Kashmir’s ethereal charm.
Mughal Gardens – Walk through timeless elegance in these Persian-style gardens, filled with cascading fountains and vibrant flowers.
Sonamarg’s Thajiwas Glacier – Experience a winter wonderland, where icy landscapes and adventure come alive.
Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend to breathtaking heights, surrounded by panoramic views of endless snow-capped mountains.
Martand Sun Temple – Marvel at the ancient ruins, where history, spirituality, and stunning architecture intertwine.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh: Land at 3,500 m and acclimatize.

  2. Leh → Nubra Valley: Cross Khardung La, the world’s highest motorable pass.

  3. Nubra Valley → Pangong Lake: Drive along the Shayok River to the blue waters of Pangong.

  4. Pangong Lake → Leh: Return via Hemis Monastery for an overnight in Leh.

  5. Leh → Kargil: Scenic transfer along the Indus River.

  6. Kargil → Srinagar: 210 km drive through mountain passes into the Kashmir Valley.

  7. Srinagar → Gulmarg → Srinagar: Day trip to Gulmarg and back.

  8. Depart Srinagar: Transfer to airport or railway for onward journey.

After an early breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Nubra Valley via Khardungla Pass (18,380 ft), the world’s highest motorable road and the gateway to the Siachen Glacier. Enjoy breathtaking panoramic views from the top of the pass. Accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria, visit Deskit Monastery (118 km), founded by Lama Sherab Zangpo in 1420 A.D. Upon arrival, check in at your pre-arranged camp or hotel. Later, visit Hundar village (7 km from Deskit) and experience the unique Double-Humped Bactrian Camel Safari in the mesmerizing sand dunes between Hundar and Deskit villages. Dinner and overnight stay at a hotel or camp in Nubra.

After breakfast at the hotel, embark on a scenic drive to Pangong Lake via Shyok, Tangtse, and Spangmik, accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria. This route is a breathtaking shortcut offering mesmerizing landscapes. The road follows the Shyok River, ascending through rugged mountains before descending into Shayok village, a picturesque settlement surrounded by barren yet stunning terrain. Enjoy the beauty of this serene village before continuing your journey. By the afternoon, arrive at the magnificent Pangong Lake, where the crystal-clear blue waters and the majestic mountains create a surreal landscape. Spend the rest of the day soaking in the beauty of the lake. Overnight stay at a camp near Pangong Lake.

After an early breakfast at 6:00 AM, embark on a scenic drive to Srinagar, accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria. En route, pass through Drass, the second coldest inhabited place on earth, where you will witness spectacular views of the famous Kargil War Peaks – Tiger Hill and Tololing Peak. Stop at the Vijay Smarak, a war memorial dedicated to the brave Indian soldiers who sacrificed their lives during the Kargil War in 1999, and pay tribute to their valor. Continue the journey through mesmerizing landscapes and reach Srinagar by late afternoon. Upon arrival, check in at your hotel and relax. Overnight stay at the hotel in Srinagar.

In the morning, proceed for Gulmarg, also known as the \"Meadow of Flowers\", situated 2,730 meters above sea level. Accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria, embark on a scenic 60 km drive, which takes approximately 2 hours. Gulmarg is renowned for having one of the best ski slopes in the world and the highest golf course with 18 holes. If the weather permits, marvel at the stunning view of Nanga Parbat, the \"Naked Mountain\", standing at over 26,000 feet, dominating the entire region. Experience an exhilarating cable car ride (Gondola) to Khilangmarg, offering breathtaking panoramic views. In the evening, drive back to Srinagar and unwind at your accommodation. Dinner and overnight stay at the hotel or a deluxe houseboat in Srinagar.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Srinagar Airport – Begin your journey amidst the lush valleys of Kashmir.

  2. Srinagar – Sonamarg – Kargil (250 km / 7–8 hrs) – Scenic drive via Zoji La to reach Kargil.

  3. Kargil – Leh (6–7 hrs) – Drive through Lamayuru and Fotu La to reach Leh.

  4. Leh – Hemis – Thiksey – Shey – 3 Idiots School – Sindhu Darshan – Explore monasteries, heritage sites, and cultural landmarks.

  5. Leh – Khardung La – Nubra Valley – Hunder Sand Dunes (120 km / 4–5 hrs) – Cross the world’s highest motorable road into desert terrain.

  6. Visit Diskit Monastery – Explore the 515-year-old monastery and giant Maitreya Buddha.

  7. Nubra – Pangong Lake (155 km) – Drive via Shayok to the mesmerizing high-altitude lake.

  8. Pangong – Leh + Leh Palace & Shanti Stupa – Return via Chang La and enjoy panoramic views in Leh.

  9. Depart Leh – Transfer to airport for your onward journey.

Sonamarg Kargil 7/8 Hrs drive 250 Kms","day_summary":"

Depart from Srinagar as per road opening timings, accompanied by our Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide. The drive to Sonamarg unveils the spectacular countryside of Kashmir in the Sindh Valley. The Sindhu Valley, the largest tributary of the Kashmir Valley, offers breathtaking views. Continue the journey towards Sonamarg (2740 Mtrs), with a scenic drive through panoramic villages. From Sonamarg, the road becomes rougher, passing through the wet Zojila Pass (3527 Mtrs), known as the Gateway to Ladakh. Continue towards Drass, the second coldest inhabited place in the world. After another two-and-a-half-hour drive, we will arrive in Kargil (2710 Mtrs), a town that gained prominence after Ladakh opened to tourism in 1974.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner)

to Leh 6/7 Hrs drive","day_summary":"

Depart from Kargil after an early breakfast, accompanied by our Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide. En route, visit the Mulbekh Monastery and Lamayuru Monastery, famous for its Moonlandscape, passing through Namkila and Fatula Passes, the highest points on the Srinagar-Leh road. Continue the drive towards Leh, stopping at the 1000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in the Ladakh region situated on flat ground. Proceed to Likir Monastery, where you’ll be awestruck by the splendid three-storey Dharma Wheel Gompa and the massive Buddha statues.

Next, visit the Indus & Zanskar River Sangam, and continue driving along the Indus River to visit Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and Magnetic Hill, where the cars defy gravity. Further on, stop at the Leh Hall of Fame, which houses a museum dedicated to the Kargil War memorabilia.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner)

After a leisurely breakfast, our Travelxploria representative, along with our experienced tour guide, will accompany you for a visit to Hemis Monastery, located 45 km west of Leh. Hemis is the largest and wealthiest monastery in Ladakh. After exploring Hemis, you will drive back on the same route to visit Thiksey Monastery, perched on a hillock with stunning views of the Indus Valley. Thiksey is renowned for its massive seated statue of Maitreya Buddha and its Dukhang assembly hall, which houses hundreds of prayer books, stacked between wooden covers and bound in silk.

Next, continue your journey through a series of chortens to visit Shey Palace, the former summer palace of the Kings of Ladakh. Later, you will visit the 3 Idiots School Campus and Sindhu Darshan.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner)

packages.package_name: Beyond the Blue: Kashmir’s Hidden Wonders
packages.overview: With Travelxploria, your Kashmir sojourn unfolds in unforgettable style—glide across the mirror-like waters of Dal Lake aboard a traditional houseboat, lulled by the gentle sway and framed by snow-tipped peaks, then wander into the vibrant heart of Srinagar to roam the Mughal Gardens’ floral splendor and lose yourself in the winding lanes of the Old City. Next, ascend to Gulmarg’s pristine slopes, where snow-clad meadows beckon both thrill-seekers and those craving serene alpine panoramas, before tracing the golden valleys from Sonmarg to Kargil—each bend revealing more of the Himalayas’ dramatic grandeur. Pay homage at the solemn Kargil War Memorial, then step into the tranquil grounds of Rangdum Monastery, its ancient walls set against rugged peaks that seem to touch the sky. Finally, Travelxploria ensures a seamless departure, leaving you with a heart full of mountain magic and stories to last a lifetime.

packages.package_name: Echoes of Paradise: Kashmir to Leh Expedition
packages.overview:
• Dal Lake Shikara Ride – Glide over shimmering waters, surrounded by floating gardens and Srinagar’s breathtaking, tranquil landscapes.
  • Mughal Gardens & Shankaracharya Temple – Wander through lush, symmetrical gardens before admiring panoramic views from a sacred hilltop temple.
  • Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend through crisp mountain air to snow-laden peaks, soaking in spectacular, alpine scenery.
  • Sonmarg & Thajiwas Glacier – Trek through pristine, icy wonderlands framed by towering, majestic mountains.
  • Srinagar to Leh via Kargil – Journey through mesmerizing, rugged terrain, capturing Ladakh’s historic and breathtaking mountain passes.
  • Nubra Valley & Hunder Dunes – Ride double-humped camels across golden, windswept sand dunes amid striking, dramatic landscapes.
  • Pangong Lake Escape – Marvel at crystal-clear, ever-changing blue waters reflecting Ladakh’s surreal, high-altitude serenity.
  • Magnetic Hill & Gurudwara Pathar Sahib – Witness mind-bending optical illusions before finding spiritual tranquility in a sacred, peaceful haven.
  • Hemis & Alchi Monasteries – Immerse in Ladakh’s rich, monastic heritage, admiring ancient frescoes and sacred relics.
  • Leh Market Stroll – Discover vibrant, handcrafted treasures, capturing the essence of Ladakh’s artistic and cultural charm.

packages.overview:
Dal Lake Houseboat Stay – Experience luxury on Srinagar’s tranquil waters, surrounded by enchanting mountain reflections and floating gardens.
Sonamarg Excursion – Marvel at golden meadows, glacial streams, and snow-capped peaks in this breathtaking valley.
Thajiwas Glacier Adventure – Explore a pristine wonderland of snow and ice, with panoramic views of majestic mountains.
Manasbal Lake Retreat – Discover Kashmir’s deepest lake, a hidden gem reflecting the serene beauty of the surrounding landscapes.
Verinag Spring – Admire the octagonal marvel with crystal-clear waters, embraced by lush pine forests and vibrant flowers.
Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend into the clouds for mesmerizing views of verdant meadows and towering Himalayan peaks.
Yousmarg Meadows – Wander through lush green pastures, surrounded by dense pine forests and awe-inspiring snow-capped peaks.
Mughal Gardens Tour – Stroll through Nishat and Shalimar Bagh, where cascading fountains and symmetrical terraces create timeless beauty.
Pahalgam Valley – Embrace nature’s charm at the confluence of Lidder River, amid rolling meadows and pine-covered slopes.
Baisaren Village – A peaceful retreat nestled in towering fir and pine forests, offering untouched tranquility and rustic charm.

Explore the beauty of this tranquil spot before returning to Srinagar in the evening for a comfortable overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Day trip to Verinag & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

The sparkling clear waters reflect the calm beauty of the area, creating a peaceful atmosphere. After exploring this natural wonder, return to Srinagar in the evening for an overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Day trip to Gulmarg & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

Early in the morning, drive through willow trees, fast-flowing streams, and lush meadows towards Gulmarg, the \"Meadow of Flowers.\" Surrounded by towering snow-capped peaks and dense coniferous forests, it offers stunning views at every turn. Stroll or take a pony ride to the world’s highest golf course, and experience the thrill of the Gondola lift. After a day filled with natural beauty and adventure, return to Srinagar in the evening for a overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Day trip to Yousmarg & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, drive to Yousmarg, located 50 km from Srinagar in the Badgam district. The two-hour drive is a treat, passing through lush green meadows, dense pine forests, and breathtaking snow-capped mountains. Yousmarg, with its peaceful ambiance, is perfect for picnics and long walks amidst nature’s beauty. In the evening, return to Srinagar for an overnight stay at your hotel.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Srinagar Local Sightseeing & Stay at Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, set off to explore the Mughal gardens of Srinagar Nishat Bagh and Shalimar Bagh, both nestled on the banks of Dal Lake. Nishat Bagh, the larger garden, boasts a beautiful terrace lined with Chinar trees. These gardens are masterpieces of symmetrical design, with central waterways and fountains dividing the lush terraces. Return to your hotel in Srinagar for the night.

You can relax on the deck, admire the breathtaking views, or just enjoy the comfort of your distinctive lodging. This serene getaway provides a wonderful introduction to the beauty of Kashmir. Overnight stay on the houseboat.

After a delightful breakfast, take an optional Shikara ride on Dal Lake, offering a peaceful start to your day. Visit Nishat Bagh, the \"Abode of Pleasure,\" followed by the enchanting Shalimar Bagh. These stunning gardens offer picturesque views with the Zabarwan Mountains in the background. On the way, explore a carpet-weaving factory before returning to your deluxe houseboat for an overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Sonamarg Day Tour","day_summary":"

After breakfast, enjoy an optional Shikara ride on Dal Lake before embarking on a full-day excursion to Sonmarg, the \"Meadow of Gold.\" Nestled in the Sindh Valley, Sonmarg offers stunning views of snow-capped mountains, alpine meadows, and lush forests. This peaceful retreat is perfect for photography and offers optional horse rides. After a delightful lunch, return to Srinagar and an overnight stay on your houseboat.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Gulmarg Day Tour","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Gulmarg, the \"Meadow of Gold,\" discovered by the British in the 19th century. Known for its stunning ski slopes and the world’s highest golf course, it offers breath-taking views of Nanga Parbat (weather permitting). Drive from Tangmarg to Gulmarg, and you can enjoy a short trek to Khilanmarg or take a Gondola ride to Kongdori. After a day, return to Srinagar and relax with an overnight stay on the houseboat.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Pahalgam Sightseeing","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark on an exciting excursion to Pahalgam, passing through Pampora's saffron fields and the historic Awantipura ruins. The journey leads you through dense pine forests and countryside to the serene confluence of Lidder River and Sheshnag Lake. Enjoy a local lunch, followed by bridle walks and photo opportunities with stunning mountain views.Take a horse ride in Pahalgam. Return to Srinagar for a relaxing overnight stay on the houseboat.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Visit to the Dachingham National Park","day_summary":"

After breakfast, head for Dachigam National Park. The Park is home to diverse wildlife and lush landscapes. Divided into lower and upper areas, it’s especially stunning in spring when apricots, peaches, and plums blossom. Enjoy a safari through this natural haven before returning to Srinagar. Check-in at your houseboat for a peaceful overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure from Srinagar","day_summary":"

After breakfast, you will be transferred to the Srinagar Airport for your onward journey. As you bid farewell to the enchanting landscapes of Kashmir, take with your unforgettable memories of the serene lakes, majestic mountains, and tranquil valleys.

packages.package_name: Kashmir Chronicles: A Perfect Blend of Tranquility and Adventure
packages.overview:
Vaishno Devi Temple – Embark on a soul-stirring pilgrimage through scenic trails, embracing divine energy and spiritual serenity.
Aru Valley – Wander through a tranquil paradise of lush meadows, snow-kissed peaks, and enchanting natural beauty.
Betaab Valley – Immerse in a cinematic dreamland of vibrant greenery, crystal-clear streams, and majestic mountains.
Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend to breathtaking heights, revealing panoramic snow-covered landscapes and thrilling adventures.
Dal Lake Shikara Ride – Glide across still waters, surrounded by floating gardens and timeless Kashmiri charm.
Thajiwas Glacier – Experience eternal snow, where adventure and mesmerizing alpine beauty unite.
Mughal Gardens – Stroll through regal terraces, vibrant blooms, and cascading fountains, reflecting timeless elegance.
Shankaracharya Temple – Bask in divine peace atop a hill, offering stunning city and lake views.

Pahalgam, located on the banks of the Lidder and Sheshnag rivers, is a calm resort famed for its peaceful atmosphere and magnificent natural beauty. It's an excellent place to relax and unwind, as well as the starting point for hikes and the Amarnath pilgrimage. Overnight stay at Pahalgam.

The environment it offers for tourists is peaceful tranquillity, while all of the exhibited beauty in the valley reveals a captivating spectacle. Further, one can visit Betab valley. The valley is surrounded with lush green meadows, snow clad mountain and dense forest. Then to Chandanwari-the base camp for Amarnath yatra. Later back to Pahalgam drive. Afternoon one can visit Basaran valley by pony it is in fact a very attractive meadow and also a good camping site for trekker looking to move further to Tulian lake, overnight stay at Pahalgam.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Visit to Gulmarg ","day_summary":"

After breakfast, take a drive to Gulmarg, where you'll pass by the Mattan Temple, Pampore's saffron fields, a cricket bat factory, and lush apple orchards, also known as the \"Meadow of Flowers,\" features charming old British-era hotels. It's renowned for having the world's highest 18-hole golf course and a popular ski resort, providing breath-taking views and plenty of adventure. After lunch, you can enjoy a Gondola ride over the snow-covered slopes, or choose to go skiing, take pony rides, or try ice skating. Spend some leisure time before settling in for the night. Overnight stay at Pahalgam.

Often referred to as the \"Meadow of Gold,\" Sonamarg showcases stunning views of the Sind Valley, featuring its lush meadows and majestic snow-capped peaks. Make sure to stop by the Thajiwas Glacier, where snow can be found year-round. This area serves as a starting point for many trekking routes and is encircled by thick forests and towering mountains. In the late afternoon, head back to Srinagar and overnight stay.

Visit the Mughal Gardens Nishat, Shalimar, and Cheshma Shahi each with stunning flower beds, fountains, and views of snow-capped peaks. If time permits, visit the Hazratbal Shrine. The day concludes with leisure time for personal activities and shopping, allowing you to take in the charm of Srinagar at your own pace. Overnight stay at Srinagar.

Shankaracharya Temple – Witness panoramic cityscapes from this ancient hilltop shrine, radiating spiritual charm and tranquility.
Mughal Gardens – Stroll through lush terraced gardens, where fountains and vibrant blooms create a timeless Persian paradise.
Dal Lake Shikara Ride – Glide across crystal-clear waters, surrounded by floating gardens and enchanting houseboats.
Pahalgam’s Lidder River – Relax by the pristine riverbanks, enveloped by lush meadows and majestic peaks.
Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Soar above snow-laden slopes, unveiling breathtaking Himalayan panoramas.

packages.overview:
Golden Temple – Witness the shimmering beauty of this sacred site, where spirituality and serenity merge in divine harmony.
Jallianwala Bagh – Step into history at this poignant memorial, echoing tales of courage and sacrifice.
Wagah Border Ceremony – Feel the patriotic fervor as India and Pakistan’s guards perform the electrifying Beating Retreat ceremony.
Vaishno Devi Trek – Embark on a soul-stirring pilgrimage through breathtaking mountain trails to seek blessings at the revered shrine.
Betaab Valley – Stroll through lush meadows surrounded by snow-capped peaks, a scene straight out of a fairytale.
Dal Lake Shikara Ride – Drift across glistening waters on a traditional wooden boat, absorbing Kashmir’s ethereal charm.
Mughal Gardens – Walk through timeless elegance in these Persian-style gardens, filled with cascading fountains and vibrant flowers.
Sonamarg’s Thajiwas Glacier – Experience a winter wonderland, where icy landscapes and adventure come alive.
Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend to breathtaking heights, surrounded by panoramic views of endless snow-capped mountains.
Martand Sun Temple – Marvel at the ancient ruins, where history, spirituality, and stunning architecture intertwine.

packages.overview:
Dal Lake – Stroll along its serene shores, where golden sunsets paint the sky and waters in a mesmerizing symphony of colors.
Mughal Gardens – Wander through lush terraces, cascading fountains, and historic charm, where nature and artistry blend in perfect harmony.
Gulmarg Gondola – Ascend to snowy heights on one of the world’s highest cable cars, unveiling breathtaking Himalayan vistas.
Betaab Valley – Embrace the tranquil beauty of this picturesque paradise, where lush meadows meet crystal-clear streams.
Thajiwas Glacier – Experience the thrill of snowball fights and pony rides in this majestic alpine wonderland.
Drung Waterfall – Hike through lush forests to discover a cascading marvel, where nature’s music fills the air.
Lal Chowk Market – Immerse in Kashmir’s vibrant culture, browsing handwoven Pashminas, aromatic spices, and exquisite handicrafts.
Hazratbal Shrine – Find peace at this sacred lakeside shrine, offering spiritual serenity and breathtaking views of Dal Lake.
Aru Valley – Unwind in its quiet ambiance, surrounded by pristine landscapes and towering peaks.

packages.overview:
​Dal Lake – Glide on a peaceful shikara ride, surrounded by snow-capped peaks and traditional houseboats in Srinagar’s serene waters.
Awantipura Ruins – Discover the ancient stone carvings of this 9th-century temple, whispering tales of Kashmir’s rich heritage.
Betaab Valley – Wander through lush meadows and crystal-clear streams, where Bollywood’s romance meets breathtaking landscapes.
Gulmarg Gondola – Soar above snow-laden slopes on one of the world’s highest cable cars, unveiling majestic mountain views.
Mughal Gardens – Stroll through terraced gardens, where cascading fountains and vibrant flowers frame Dal Lake’s stunning beauty.
Tulip Garden – If in season, witness a riot of colors, as thousands of tulips bloom against the backdrop of Kashmir’s hills.
Thajiwas Glacier – Embark on an adventurous pony ride to this snowy paradise, embraced by towering Himalayan peaks.
Lal Chowk Market – Immerse yourself in Kashmir’s bustling culture, shopping for handwoven shawls, saffron, and exquisite handicrafts.
Shankaracharya Temple – Climb to this sacred hilltop shrine, where panoramic city views and a spiritual aura await.

packages.overview:
Shankaracharya Temple – Witness breathtaking panoramic views of Srinagar and Dal Lake from this sacred hilltop shrine.
Nishat & Shalimar Bagh – Stroll through vibrant Mughal gardens, adorned with blooming flowers and tranquil fountains.
Lal Chowk Market – Explore bustling streets filled with exquisite Kashmiri handicrafts, intricate shawls, and aromatic dried fruits.
Thajiwas Glacier, Sonmarg – Experience a mesmerizing landscape of shimmering snow and untouched alpine beauty.

packages.overview: With Travelxploria, your Kashmir odyssey unfolds in a tapestry of emerald lakes, snow-draped vistas, and verdant valleys. Begin by gliding across Dal Lake’s mirror-like waters in a traditional shikara, framed by the lofty Pir Panjal Range and dotted with floating gardens. Next, ascend to new heights on the Gulmarg Gondola—Asia’s highest cable car—where panoramic views of snow-clad peaks give way to exhilarating snow activities at every turn. Journey onward to the Pahalgam Valleys, wandering through the lush meadows of Aru, the cinematic landscapes of Betaab, and the fairy-tale groves of Baisaran. Then, step into a living poem at the Mughal Gardens, where terraced lawns, tinkling fountains, and vibrant flowerbeds whisper stories of royal retreat. Venture off the beaten path to the hidden haven of Doodhpathri Meadows, a serene expanse of rolling green carpeted with pine and birch. Finally, let Travelxploria guide you to Sonmarg’s Thajiwas Glacier, where sparkling ice fields beckon for scenic treks beneath the grandeur of the Himalayas—leaving you with heart-stirring memories of Kashmir’s timeless splendor.

packages.overview:
Dal Lake – Glide on a serene Shikara ride, surrounded by floating gardens and majestic snow-capped mountains.
Mughal Gardens – Wander through lush terraced lawns, vibrant flowers, and historic fountains, reflecting Mughal grandeur.
Shankaracharya Temple – Admire panoramic city views from this sacred hilltop temple, a place of spiritual tranquillity.
Gulmarg Gondola – Ascend on one of the world’s highest cable cars, witnessing breathtaking snowy landscapes.
Sonmarg – Experience pristine valleys, crystal-clear rivers, and majestic peaks, a peaceful retreat in nature’s lap.
Vaishno Devi – Embark on a divine pilgrimage, feeling sacred energy amidst the Trikuta Mountains.
Jammu Markets – Explore bustling streets filled with colorful handicrafts, aromatic spices, and cultural souvenirs.

packages.overview: With Travelxploria, step into a royal tapestry of Kashmir’s most spellbinding landscapes. Stroll beneath the latticed pavilions and cascading chinar trees of Shalimar Bagh and Nishat Bagh—Mughal Gardens where artful terraces and fragrant blooms whisper tales of emperors past. Glide serenely across Dal Lake in a traditional shikara, drifting among floating gardens as snow-draped peaks stand sentinel on the horizon. In Betaab Valley, wander through emerald meadows punctuated by crystal-clear streams, discovering a true alpine paradise at the foot of lofty mountains. Then, breathe in the pristine air of Aru Valley, where rolling hills and snow-capped summits beckon you into Kashmir’s untamed heart. Ascend on the Gulmarg Gondola for panoramic vistas of a winter wonderland, and finally, pay homage at Chandanwari—the sacred gateway to the Amarnath Yatra—where rugged beauty meets spiritual serenity. Each moment with Travelxploria is curated to immerse you in the timeless allure of Kashmir’s crown jewels.

overview:
Best Tour Packages for Kashmir from India – Experience Heaven on Earth


If you're searching for the best tour packages for Kashmir from India, you’ve arrived at the perfect guide. Kashmir, often referred to as "Heaven on Earth", offers a stunning blend of snow-capped mountains, serene lakes, and timeless charm. Whether you're planning a honeymoon, family trip, or adventure tour, our Kashmir Tour Packages from cities like Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, and Pune promise a once-in-a-lifetime experience.

Why Visit Kashmir?


Snow-Capped Mountains and Scenic Valleys

The majestic Himalayas cradle Kashmir’s valleys in beauty. From the snowy slopes of Gulmarg to the tranquil backwaters of Dal Lake, nature showcases its grandeur in every season. Visit during April to June for blooming tulips and lush meadows; visit in December to February for snow-covered landscapes and winter sports.


Rich Culture, Handicrafts, and Cuisine

Top Kashmir Tour Packages from India
​Explore authentic Kashmiri culture through vibrant local bazaars, saffron fields, and traditional Wazwan feasts. Pashmina shawls, handwoven carpets, copperware, and dry fruits make for excellent souvenirs. Popular markets like Lal Chowk and Polo View Market in Srinagar offer unique shopping experiences.
​
Ideal Destination for Honeymoon, Family, and Adventure Tours
Our Kashmir family tour packages, affordable Kashmir tour packages for couples, and adventure-based itineraries ensure every traveler finds their dream holiday. From peaceful houseboats to adrenaline-filled ski trips, there’s something for all.


Top Kashmir Tour Packages from India


4 Days Kashmir Tour Package from India – Perfect Short Getaway

This short itinerary includes Srinagar’s iconic Dal Lake, Mughal Gardens, Shankaracharya Temple, and a half-day excursion to Gulmarg. It’s perfect for a quick trip from cities like Kolkata or Pune.


6 Days Kashmir Family Tour Package – Explore Srinagar to Pahalgam
Tailored for families, this tour covers Srinagar, Gulmarg, Pahalgam, and optional add-ons like Betaab Valley and Aru Valley. This package is popular among travelers from Mumbai and Delhi.


Romantic Kashmir Honeymoon Packages from India
Designed for couples, these budget-friendly Kashmir honeymoon packages include private houseboat stays, candlelight dinners, shikara rides, and romantic strolls through flower gardens. Custom add-ons like floral bedroom decor and snow activities are available.


Budget and Luxury Kashmir Tour Packages for Indian Travelers
Choose from basic budget itineraries to luxury all-inclusive experiences. Kashmir tour packages with price options starting from ₹11,999 up to ₹45,000+ depending on the number of nights, season, and accommodation type.

Key Destinations Covered in Kashmir Tour Packages

Srinagar – Houseboats, Dal Lake & Mughal Gardens
Float on the Dal Lake in a traditional wooden houseboat, enjoy a shikara ride through lotus fields, and visit Mughal Gardens like Shalimar Bagh and Nishat Bagh. Don’t miss sites like Hazratbal Shrine and Pari Mahal.

Gulmarg – Snow Adventures & Cable Car Rides
A paradise for snow lovers, Gulmarg offers skiing, snowboarding, and Asia’s highest cable car ride—the Gulmarg Gondola. Best visited in winter (December to February).

Sonmarg – The Gateway to Ladakh
Surrounded by glaciers and green valleys, Sonmarg is perfect for trekking and a key base point for journeys toward Leh. Access may be limited in heavy winter months.

Inclusions in the Best Kashmir Tour Packages from India

Comfortable Accommodation & Meals
Tour packages include stays in hotels or houseboats with daily breakfast and dinner. Options range from 3-star to luxury boutique stays, with the flexibility to customize.

Local Transfers, Sightseeing, and Houseboat Stays
Packages include airport pickup/drop, private cabs or shared transfers, houseboat stays in Srinagar, and all major sightseeing with optional local guides.

Optional Add-ons: Skiing, Trekking, and Shikara Rides

Personalize your experience with thrilling add-ons like:

Gondola rides in Gulmarg
Rafting in Pahalgam
Shikara rides on Dal Lake
Snow treks and sledding in winter

Best Time to Visit Kashmir from India

Summer for Blooming Gardens and Mild Weather
April to June is the best time to enjoy the Tulip Festival, green meadows, and pleasant weather. Ideal for sightseeing and family trips.

Winter for Snow Activities and Scenic Landscapes
December to February is perfect for skiing, snowboarding, and cozy winter getaways. Great for honeymooners and adventure seekers.

Travel Tips for Each Season

Summer: Carry sunscreen, sunglasses, and light woolens
Winter: Layer up with thermals, gloves, snow boots, and jackets
Monsoon: Travel with raincoats and non-slip shoes; enjoy discounted prices

Booking 45–60 days in advance is recommended for peak seasons to secure good deals.

Frequently Asked Questions About Kashmir Tour Packages from India

Is Kashmir safe for tourists from India?
Yes, tourist areas like Srinagar, Gulmarg, and Pahalgam are safe and regularly visited. Always check official travel advisories before planning your trip.

How much does a typical Kashmir tour package cost?
Kashmir tour packages with price range from ₹11,999 (budget, 3N/4D without flights) to ₹45,000+ for deluxe stays and longer itineraries. Prices vary by season and inclusions.

Can I customize my Kashmir package based on my preferences?
Yes, all packages are customizable. You can add or remove destinations, choose hotel categories, add activities, or adjust for budget or travel style.

Are there affordable Kashmir tour packages for couples?
Yes, affordable Kashmir tour packages for couples include private stays, romantic sightseeing, and experiences like candlelight dinners and shikara rides—all within a reasonable budget.

Do you offer budget-friendly Kashmir honeymoon packages?
Yes. Our budget-friendly Kashmir honeymoon packages include comfortable hotels, honeymoon decor, houseboat stays, and couple-friendly sightseeing, starting at ₹17,999.

What about affordable Kashmir adventure tour packages?
Adventure seekers can opt for affordable Kashmir adventure tour packages with snow sports in Gulmarg, rafting in Pahalgam, and glacier treks in Sonmarg, all guided and budget-managed.

How to Book the Best Tour Package for Kashmir from India

Trusted Travel Agencies and Online Portals
Book only through verified travel agents or online platforms affiliated with the Ministry of Tourism. Look for customer reviews and transparency in inclusions.

Things to Check Before Finalizing Your Package

1. What’s included: meals, sightseeing, transport
2. Type of room: standard, deluxe, or houseboat
3. Cancellation policy and refund terms
4. Ratings and reviews of travel partners

Travel Documents and Packing Essentials

1. Valid photo ID (Aadhar, PAN, or Passport)
​2. Sufficient cash or UPI backup (rural ATMs may be limited)
3. Power bank, cold weather gear, first-aid kit
4. Foreign nationals must carry passports and may need extra registration
